note: Cooper game-type presets. Editable data — add/tune types here without recompiling. Each type prefills the New Game wizard; the user can adjust everything (or pick 'custom'). graphics.mode = SNES BG mode 0..7; graphics.objSize = OBSEL pair id 0..5 (0=8/16,1=8/32,2=8/64,3=16/32,4=16/64,5=32/64). build flags map to the Makefile USE_* knobs; the sound/sram/superfx/sa1 LIB_MODULES are added by those flags, so `modules` lists only the graphics/logic modules (deps auto-resolve at build).

types.name: Platformer / Action
types.blurb: Side-scrolling action — Mario, Mega Man, Donkey Kong Country.

types.name: RPG (turn-based)
types.blurb: Story, menus and battles — Final Fantasy, Chrono Trigger, Dragon Quest.

types.name: Shoot 'em up
types.blurb: Bullets and bosses — Gradius, R-Type, Axelay.

types.name: Fighting / Versus
types.blurb: Big characters trading blows — Street Fighter II, Killer Instinct.

types.name: Racing (Mode 7)
types.blurb: A rotating, scaling track — F-Zero, Mario Kart, Top Gear. Add the fixed32/math modules when you implement the perspective transforms.

types.name: Puzzle
types.blurb: One screen, tight rules — Tetris, Puyo Puyo, Panel de Pon.

types.name: Action-adventure
types.blurb: Explore a top-down/side world — Zelda: A Link to the Past, Secret of Mana.

types.name: Custom — I'll choose everything
types.blurb: Start from a minimal but bootable base and pick your own mode, chips and modules.
